title: Smart Contract Patterns
description: Common design patterns for building on EUTxO.

stops.title: State Machines
stops.narrative: The [state machine pattern](node:state-machine) models contract state as a [datum](node:datum). The validator checks that input datum + redeemer produces a valid output datum. Used for multi-step protocols: escrow, auctions, DEX order matching.

stops.title: Oracle Pattern
stops.narrative: The [oracle pattern](node:oracle-pattern) uses [reference inputs](node:reference-inputs) to publish data that many contracts can read without spending. Price feeds, exchange rates, random numbers — all published as UTxO datums.

stops.title: Withdraw Zero Trick
stops.narrative: The [withdraw zero pattern](node:staking-validator) is a key optimization. Instead of running a spending validator per input (O(n²) cost), delegate validation to a staking validator that runs once per transaction (O(n)). Documented by Anastasia Labs.

stops.title: Minting Policies & Token Standards
stops.narrative: [Minting policies](node:minting-policy) control token creation. [CIP-25](node:cip-25) defines immutable NFT metadata at mint time. [CIP-68](node:cip-68) adds mutable metadata via reference tokens — the standard for modern Cardano NFTs and rich fungible tokens.
